findings.issue_description: Memory ordering uses atomic_thread_fence with separate load/store operations. While functionally correct, C++17 provides stronger guarantees with atomic operations using read-modify-write semantics (fetch_add, compare_exchange_weak) which could simplify the memory ordering model.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: The current implementation is correct per C++17 atomics. The fence + store pattern provides equivalent guarantees to a release store. However, for SPSC where only one thread writes head, the current approach is optimal.

security_considerations: Lock-free algorithms must be verified for ABA problems - not applicable here due to ring buffer design

findings.issue_description: The wait() function uses recursion on EINTR (line 208: 'return wait()'). While POSIX-compliant, deep recursion could theoretically cause stack overflow under signal pressure. Iterative approach is safer for real-time systems.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Iterative EINTR handling eliminates recursion depth concerns while maintaining POSIX compliance. Recommended for safety-critical real-time systems.

findings.issue_description: Thread priority 99 (SCHED_FIFO max) is used for safety_monitor. Per POSIX.1b, priorities 1-99 are valid for SCHED_FIFO, but priority 99 should be reserved for critical safety functions only. Current usage is appropriate.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Priority assignment follows real-time best practices: safety-critical functions get highest priority. The hierarchy ensures safety monitor can preempt all other threads.

security_considerations: SCHED_FIFO threads can starve lower-priority threads - ensure proper priority hierarchy
Root privileges required for SCHED_FIFO priority > 0

findings.issue_description: clock_nanosleep() with TIMER_ABSTIME is correctly used for drift-free periodic wakeup. This is the recommended POSIX pattern for real-time periodic threads.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Absolute time sleep (TIMER_ABSTIME) is the correct pattern for periodic real-time threads. Relative sleep would accumulate timing errors over multiple cycles.

alternatives: timer_create() + timer_settime() for signal-based timing (more complex, not recommended)

findings.issue_description: KCF tracker is used instead of CSRT. Per QWEN.md, CSRT was specified (AM7-L2-VIS-008), but KCF is chosen for performance (1-2ms vs 10-20ms). This is a documented trade-off but deviates from the specified algorithm.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: KCF provides 10x speedup over CSRT but lacks scale invariance and has lower accuracy. The trade-off is documented but represents a spec deviation (AM7-L2-VIS-008 specifies CSRT).

findings.issue_description: cv::matchTemplate() is used for redetection in KcfTracker::redetect(). This is O(n*m) complexity and could exceed WCET budget for large search regions. Bounded search ROI is implemented but should be monitored.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Search ROI bounding is correctly implemented. The 100px margin limits worst-case complexity. For production, consider feature-based redetection (ORB matching) for O(log n) complexity.

alternatives: Correlation filter peak analysis (faster, less accurate)

findings.issue_description: The vision thread uses std::cerr for logging deadline misses (line 198). std::cerr involves heap allocation and I/O operations which are NOT real-time safe. This violates AM7-L3-TIM-002 (no heap allocation after init).

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Real-time threads must not perform I/O or heap allocation. Use a lock-free ring buffer to queue log messages for async processing by a non-RT thread.

security_considerations: Heap allocation in RT threads can cause unbounded latency due to memory fragmentation
I/O operations can block indefinitely on congested systems
alternatives: Pre-allocated circular buffer with async writer
Shared memory ring buffer for external monitoring

findings.issue_description: main() uses std::cout for status output in the main loop (line 120+). While main thread is not RT-critical, the status loop runs concurrently with RT threads and could cause priority inversion if stdout is contended.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Status output should be isolated from RT thread scheduling. A dedicated status thread or lock-free telemetry writer prevents any potential priority inversion.

findings.issue_description: SafetyMonitor uses std::thread for the watchdog thread. std::thread does not guarantee real-time scheduling inheritance. The watchdog thread should explicitly set SCHED_FIFO priority 99 after creation.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: std::thread inherits the creator's scheduling policy. For safety-critical watchdog, explicitly set SCHED_FIFO priority 99 to ensure deterministic response to faults.

security_considerations: Watchdog must have highest priority to detect and respond to faults
Priority inheritance prevents unbounded priority inversion

findings.issue_description: StateMachine uses std::function for state change callback (line 85). std::function may allocate memory on construction/assignment, violating AM7-L3-TIM-002. Should use pre-allocated callback or function pointer.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Function pointers with user_data avoid heap allocation. If type-erasure is needed, use a pre-allocated std::function with small buffer optimization (SBO) or custom allocator.

findings.issue_description: LockFreeRingBuffer uses alignas(CACHE_LINE_SIZE) for cache-line alignment. This is correct per QWEN.md requirements. CACHE_LINE_SIZE=64 is appropriate for ARM Cortex-A72 (RPi 5).

correct_usage_pattern.code_example:
// CORRECT implementation:
constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE_SIZE = 64;  // Cortex-A72 cache line

alignas(CACHE_LINE_SIZE) std::atomic<uint32_t> head_;  // Producer writes
alignas(CACHE_LINE_SIZE) std::atomic<uint32_t> tail_;  // Consumer writes
alignas(CACHE_LINE_SIZE) T buffer_[Size];  // Data array
correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Cache-line alignment prevents false sharing between producer (head) and consumer (tail) variables. This is critical for lock-free performance on multi-core systems.

findings.issue_description: The track_compute thread has a TODO stub (line 230) with no actual tracking implementation. Per QWEN.md, this is expected (TODO stub), but represents an incomplete implementation blocking AM7-L2-VIS-008 compliance.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: The tracking implementation is marked as TODO in QWEN.md. This is a known gap blocking compliance_complete gate. The regression tests (test_regression_002_csrt_tracker.cpp) are prepared for this feature.

findings.issue_description: CameraWrapper has a stub implementation (returns invalid frames). Per QWEN.md, this is expected - actual libcamera integration requires Raspberry Pi hardware. The zero-copy design (DMA mmap + cv::Mat header wrapping) is correctly architected.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: The zero-copy architecture is correctly designed. Actual implementation requires libcamera integration on Raspberry Pi hardware with MIPI CSI-2 camera module.

security_considerations: DMA buffer sharing requires careful synchronization to prevent race conditions
mmap() failures must be handled gracefully

findings.issue_description: CMakeLists.txt correctly sets C++17 standard (line 45: CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17). ARM NEON flags are correctly configured (-march=armv8-a+fp+simd). Security hardening flags (-fstack-protector-strong, -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2) are present.

correct_usage_pattern.code_example:
// CORRECT CMake configuration:
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF)  # Use standard C++17, not GNU extensions

# ARM NEON:
add_compile_options(-march=armv8-a+fp+simd)

# Security hardening:
add_compile_options(-fstack-protector-strong -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2)
add_link_options(-Wl,-z,relro,-z,now -Wl,-z,noexecstack)
correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Build configuration is correct. C++17 standard is enforced, NEON SIMD is enabled for ARM targets, and security hardening flags are applied.

findings.issue_description: Multiple source files listed in CMakeLists.txt are stubs or missing: src/vision/orb_detector.cpp (partial), src/tracking/csrt_tracker.cpp (KCF not CSRT), src/actuation/ballistic_solver.cpp (basic), src/state_machine/state_machine.cpp (partial), src/common/hud_socket.cpp (missing). These represent spec compliance gaps.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: Per QWEN.md 'What Is Implemented vs. TODO', these are known gaps. The compliance_complete gate is correctly set to FAILED due to these gaps.

findings.issue_description: StageLatencyStats uses compare_exchange_weak for lock-free max update (line 145). This is correct per C++17 atomics and QWEN.md requirement for explicit memory ordering. The memory_order_relaxed ordering is appropriate for statistics counters.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: The compare-exchange loop correctly handles spurious failures. memory_order_relaxed is appropriate because statistics don't require synchronization - only eventual consistency.

findings.issue_description: LockFreeRingBuffer uses memory_order_release for head store and memory_order_acquire for tail load. This is the correct memory ordering for SPSC lock-free queues per C++17 atomics.

correct_usage_pattern.explanation: The acquire-release ordering creates a happens-before relationship: producer's data write happens-before consumer's data read. This is the canonical SPSC pattern.

alternatives: memory_order_seq_cst (stronger, slower - not needed for SPSC)

correct_usage_pattern.description: Real-time safe logging pattern using lock-free ring buffer

source_references.name: POSIX.1b Realtime Extensions
source_references.relevant_section: Real-time I/O and memory allocation constraints

known_issues: std::cerr/cout in RT threads can cause unbounded latency
Heap allocation after init violates AM7-L3-TIM-002
KCF tracker used instead of specified CSRT (performance trade-off)

compliance_rationale: Code analysis identified 17 findings across 6 categories: 2 high-severity (RT safety violations with std::cerr in RT threads), 4 medium-severity (KCF vs CSRT, RT logging, std::function allocation, missing implementations), 5 low-severity (recursion on EINTR, OpenCV complexity, state machine callback), 6 info-level (correct patterns confirmed). Critical gaps blocking compliance_complete: (1) AM7-L2-HUD-004: HUD socket missing, (2) AM7-L2-VIS-008: CSRT replaced with KCF, (3) AM7-L1-MODE-002: State machine partial, (4) AM7-L2-BALL-001: Ballistic engine basic, (5) AM7-L2-ACT-001: I2C interface stub. RT safety violations (REALTIME_001, REALTIME_002) should be fixed before deployment but do not block initial compliance gate.
